description_long: This object consists of a group of five timber-framed houses arranged in a semi-circular courtyard layout, each with steeply pitched reddish-brown gable roofs and white plaster walls accented by dark wooden beams. The buildings feature multiple chimneys emitting smoke, suggesting active fireplaces inside. Windows are symmetrically arranged, with some houses displaying double rows of windows and others single rows, all framed by dark wood. The central house has an arched passageway connecting two buildings, creating a sheltered entry into the courtyard. One house includes an exterior staircase leading to an upper level, while the others have ground-level entrances. The overall style evokes a traditional European or Tudor village aesthetic, with visible stone foundations and decorative timbering.
description: A cluster of five timber-framed, gable-roofed houses arranged around a central courtyard, featuring white plaster walls, dark wood beams, and multiple chimneys with smoke.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the rear and side profiles of the houses, highlighting the gable roofs, chimneys, and the semi-circular arrangement with a clear gap in the center.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the front of the houses, emphasizing the central arched entryway, symmetrical window placement, and the inviting courtyard layout.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the opposite rear and side angles, revealing the exterior staircase on the leftmost house and the alignment of the buildings.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a direct look into the courtyard, showcasing the main entry arch, the arrangement of windows, and the decorative timber framing.
attribute_entryway_design: Central arched passageway with timber framing, no porch, and decorative beams
